originalTitle: Deal with US not imminent, Iran says
summary: The US secretary of state earlier said that an agreement could possibly come on Monday.
content:
Iran says some progress has been reached in talks with the US, but a deal "is not imminent".

Foreign ministry spokesman Esmail Baqai's remarks came after US Secretary of State Marco Rubio said an agreement could possibly be reached on Monday.

"It is correct to say that we have reached a conclusion on a large portion of the issues under discussion," Baqai said in Tehran on Monday. "But to say that this means the signing of an agreement is imminent - no-one can make such a claim."

The memorandum of understanding reportedly involves a 60-day ceasefire extension, reopening the Strait of Hormuz and a plan for further negotiations over Iran's nuclear programme.

At the weekend, President Donald Trump suggested the sides were closing a deal, even though he later said he had instructed negotiators "not to rush into" one.

On Monday, Rubio told reporters in the Indian capital, Delhi: "We thought we might have some news last night. Maybe today."

Cautioning "I wouldn't read too much into it", Rubio said: "It takes a little while to hear back from Iran."

CBS News, the BBC's US partner, has reported that US intelligence believes Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei - who was injured in an Israeli strike on the first day of the war which killed his father and predecessor - is holed up in an undisclosed location, making communication with his envoys difficult and therefore delaying pace of talks with the US.

According to US media, the mooted deal is not a final settlement - instead it leaves some of the thorniest issues to be negotiated later, including the scope and timing of Iranian sanctions relief, the release of frozen Iranian funds, and Washington's demands for Iran to curb its nuclear ambitions.

"So we have, what I think is a pretty solid thing on the table in terms of their ability to open up the Straits," he said referring to the Strait of Hormuz - the crucial waterway where 20% of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas passes and which Iran has been blocking.

Oil prices fell sharply and Asian stock markets rose on Monday on hopes of an agreement.

Oil prices slide on hopes of US-Iran peace deal

Why and how is US blockading Iranian ports in Strait of Hormuz?

The reported deal has split Trump's Republicans, with some publicly arguing it is too lenient on Iran.

Senator Ted Cruz said it would be "a disastrous mistake", while Roger Wicker, who chairs the Senate Armed Services Committee, said a 60-day ceasefire would mean "everything accomplished by Operation Epic Fury would be for naught!"

Senator Lindsey Graham, who is a close Trump ally, also criticised any deal that would leave Iran perceived as being a dominant force in the region.

"It makes one wonder why the war started to begin with," he said.

Trump responded by dismissing his critics as "losers" and saying "the deal with Iran will either be a great and meaningful one, or there will be no deal".

Even in a best case scenario, the effects of a deal are unlikely to be seen right away.

It could be months before the shipping industry will be able to go back to supply chains that are "physically in the same shape as they were before the crisis", Lars Jensen, chief executive of Vespucci Maritime and former director of shipping company Maersk, told BBC Radio 4's Today programme.

Should a deal between Iran and the US be announced in the coming days, the industry would still remain "cautious and hesitant" to make any "major operational changes", Jensen explained.

The US and Israel launched wide-ranging strikes on Iran on 28 February, sparking conflict across the Middle East. Iran responded by attacking Israel and US-allied states in the Gulf, and effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz. The move sent oil prices soaring globally.

Shortly after a ceasefire was agreed in early April, the US established a blockade of Iranian ports, which Trump says will remain "in full force and effect until an agreement is reached, certified, and signed".

In Sunday's post on Truth Social, Trump reiterated that Iran "must understand" it cannot develop a nuclear weapon. Tehran has repeatedly said its nuclear programme is solely for peaceful purposes.

Some reports in US media suggest the deal could see Iran agree to eventually hand over its highly enriched uranium.

At the start of the war, Iran is thought to have had about 440kg (970 lbs) of uranium that was enriched up to 60% purity - a short process away from being enriched further to the weapons-grade 90%, which theoretically could allow it to create a nuclear bomb.

In an update posted to Truth Social on Monday night, Trump said that the enriched uranium would either be "immediately" turned over to the US, or "preferably, in conjunction and coordination with the Islamic Republic of Iran, destroyed in place".

Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian told state TV Iran was ready "to assure the world that we are not after a nuclear weapon."

Trump says he called off new Iran attack at request of Gulf states

Iran steps up claim to control Strait of Hormuz

I survived a missile strike in the Strait of Hormuz, but my friend has not been found
chineseTitle: 伊朗表示不会立即与美国达成协议
chineseSummary: 美国国务卿早些时候表示，可能会在周一达成协议。
chineseContent:
伊朗表示，与美国的谈判已取得一些进展，但达成协议“并非迫在眉睫”。

外交部发言人埃斯梅尔·巴盖发表此番言论之前，美国国务卿马可·卢比奥表示，周一可能达成协议。

巴盖周一在德黑兰表示：“可以说，我们已经就正在讨论的大部分问题得出了结论。” “但如果说这意味着协议的签署迫在眉睫——没有人可以做出这样的断言。”

据报道，该谅解备忘录涉及延长停火60天、重新开放霍尔木兹海峡以及就伊朗核计划进行进一步谈判的计划。

周末，唐纳德·特朗普总统暗示双方正在达成协议，尽管他后来表示，他已指示谈判代表“不要仓促达成”协议。

周一，卢比奥在印度首都德里告诉记者：“我们认为昨晚可能会得到一些消息。也许今天。”

卢比奥警告说，“我不会对此进行太多解读”，“需要一段时间才能收到伊朗的回复。”

BBC美国合作伙伴哥伦比亚广播公司新闻报道称，美国情报部门认为，伊朗最高领袖哈梅内伊躲藏在一个秘密地点，这使得与特使的沟通变得困难，从而推迟了与美国的谈判进度。哈梅内伊在战争第一天的以色列袭击中受伤，导致其父亲和前任丧生。

据美国媒体报道，拟议中的协议并不是最终的解决方案，而是将一些最棘手的问题留待以后进行谈判，包括伊朗制裁解除的范围和时间、释放冻结的伊朗资金，以及华盛顿要求伊朗遏制其核野心。

“所以，我认为就他们开放海峡的能力而言，我们已经摆在桌面上，”他指的是霍尔木兹海峡。世界上20%的石油和液化天然气都经过这条重要的水道，而伊朗一直在封锁这条海峡。

由于达成协议的希望，油价大幅下跌，亚洲股市周一上涨。

油价下跌因美伊和平协议的希望而下跌

美国为何以及如何封锁霍尔木兹海峡的伊朗港口？

报道称，特朗普的共和党人对此产生了分歧，一些人公开认为该协议对伊朗过于宽容。

参议员泰德·克鲁兹表示，这将是“一个灾难性的错误”，而参议院军事委员会主席罗杰·威克则表示，60 天的停火将意味着“史诗愤怒行动所取得的一切都将化为乌有！”

特朗普的亲密盟友、参议员林赛·格雷厄姆也批评任何会让伊朗被视为该地区主导力量的协议。

“这让人想知道战争为何开始，”他说。

特朗普回应称，他的批评者是“失败者”，并表示“与伊朗的协议要么是一项伟大而有意义的协议，要么不会达成协议”。

即使在最好的情况下，交易的效果也不太可能立即显现。

Vespucci Maritime 首席执行官、航运公司马士基前董事拉尔斯·詹森 (Lars Jensen) 告诉 BBC 广播 4 台《今日》节目，航运业可能需要几个月的时间才能恢复到“与危机前的实际状况相同”的供应链。

詹森解释说，如果伊朗和美国之间的协议在未来几天内宣布，该行业仍将对做出任何“重大运营改变”保持“谨慎和犹豫”。

2月28日，美国和以色列对伊朗发动大范围打击，引发中东冲突。伊朗的回应是攻击以色列和美国在海湾的盟国，并有效关闭了霍尔木兹海峡。此举导致全球油价飙升。

4月初停火协议达成后不久，美国就对伊朗港口实施了封锁，特朗普表示，封锁将“完全有效，直到达成协议、获得认证并签署为止”。

特朗普周日在《Truth Social》上发表的帖子重申，伊朗“必须明白”它无法发展核武器。德黑兰一再表示其核计划仅用于和平目的。

美国媒体的一些报道表明，该协议可能会让伊朗同意最终交出其高浓缩铀。

战争开始时，伊朗被认为拥有约 440 公斤（970 磅）的浓缩铀，纯度高达 60%，距离进一步浓缩至武器级 90% 仅有很短的路程，理论上伊朗可以制造核弹。

特朗普在周一晚上发布给Truth Social的最新消息中表示，浓缩铀要么“立即”移交给美国，要么“最好与伊朗伊斯兰共和国联合和协调，就地销毁”。

伊朗总统马苏德·佩泽什基安对国家电视台表示，伊朗已准备好“向世界保证，我们并不追求核武器”。

特朗普表示，他应海湾国家的要求取消了新的伊朗袭击

伊朗加强声称控制霍尔木兹海峡

我在霍尔木兹海峡的导弹袭击中幸存，但我的朋友还没有被发现
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
